The first man to cross the North Pole, Eric Larsen could hardly wait to break the final ice. The first plane to traverse the continent was moments from taking off.

All it needed was a man.

After a lifetime spent breaking records at the North Pole, climbing mountains and exploring the world, Larsen was ready for perhaps his greatest challenge ever: To bring a living man to the pole and keep him there for two weeks.
